danger_signals.visual_form: Armor depletion indicator; craft damage accumulation on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Player helicopter taking damage from hostile forces; immediate threat to mission success

danger_signals.visual_form: Fuel meter declining; eventual craft crash if depleted
danger_signals.meaning: Resource scarcity creating time pressure and mission failure condition

danger_signals.visual_form: Destructible enemy structures and turrets positioned across map
danger_signals.meaning: Active hostile threats that must be neutralized to progress safely

danger_signals.visual_form: Massive destruction in targeted cities (New York depicted with mass chaos)
danger_signals.meaning: Critical story escalation; Malone's threat becoming lethal reality

safety_signals.visual_form: NPCs airlifted to landing zones; armor replenishment upon delivery
safety_signals.meaning: Successful mission completion; restoration of player resources through humanitarian action
Resource availability; player agency in managing combat sustainability

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated safe extraction points on map
safety_signals.meaning: Mission waypoints; safe havens for refueling and hostage delivery

safety_signals.visual_form: Fuel barrels placed strategically in level environments
safety_signals.meaning: Mission sustainability; player can extend operational range

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Friendly forces; helicopter and ground support units visually distinct from enemy combatants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military installations allied to player; landing zones and command centers

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy combatants, assassins, armed personnel visually differentiated from player forces
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Superweapon facility near Las Vegas; weapons installations across Hawaii, Mexico, San Francisco; assassination operations in Washington DC

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Hostages and prisoners requiring rescue; plastic surgeon NPC in Washington DC
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban centers (New York damaged by superweapon); rescue mission locations across North American continent

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Early campaign - Malone gathering superweapon components
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hawaii environment; theft operations visible; assassination attempts on civilian targets
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy forces conducting resource acquisition missions; hostages under threat requiring rescue

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mexico and San Francisco missions; increased military installations and weapon emplacements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy defenses intensifying; multi-stage mission complexity increasing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: New York City depicted with catastrophic damage from superweapon impacts; chaos and destruction visible across urban landscape
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Shift to damage control and civilian rescue; player forced into reactive survival mode

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Las Vegas desert facility location revealed; superweapon control center as final objective
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Malone's last stand; bomb detonation sequence; destruction of both antagonist and weapon in climactic encounter

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Interior environments accessible via on-foot missions; shift from helicopter-only to mixed vehicle/infantry gameplay
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player dismounts helicopter; uses rifle and missile launcher in ground-based combat; different tactical approach to objectives

environmental_storytelling.example: Plastic surgeon rescued in Hawaii mission reveals Malone's true identity as Carlos Ortega (presumed dead from Jungle Strike); gameplay rescue directly enables story revelation

Superweapon construction across multiple mission locations reflects Malone's power consolidation; geographical progression (Hawaii → Mexico → San Francisco → Las Vegas) shows supply chain and influence expansion
New York City's visual destruction from superweapon strikes demonstrates threat credibility; environmental damage shows stakes have escalated beyond combat to civilian casualties
Early missions focus on component theft prevention; later missions shift to damage mitigation and facility infiltration, mirroring narrative progression from prevention to crisis response
Campaign spanning Hawaii, Mexico, San Francisco, Washington DC, New York, and Las Vegas demonstrates Malone's continental network; diverse locales show distributed threat infrastructure

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Hidden identity; operational through distributed agents; assassination tactics against witnesses
Aggressive; multiple weapon deployments; willing to cause mass civilian casualties; tracked through weapon fire location signatures
Desperate; suicide-bomb attempt; killed by own superweapon detonation
Covert intelligence gathering for Strike C.O.R.E.; cover blown to Malone; killed early via car-bomb
Defend weapons installations; patrol mission areas; respond to player incursion with escalating force
Immobile; vulnerable; require extraction to designated zones; provide armor reward upon successful delivery

notes: Urban Strike communicates world-state primarily through mission progression tied to geographical locations and escalating threat visualization. Danger manifests through resource scarcity (fuel, ammo, armor) and enemy opposition intensity. Political situation emerges through NPC dialogue and superweapon revelation mechanics rather than explicit faction visual design. The game's manifestation layer relies on environmental destruction (particularly New York's damage) to signal narrative stakes escalation. Vehicle switching (Mohican, Blackhawke, GAV) and on-foot missions introduce mechanical state changes that reflect gameplay variety but limited visual faction differentiation. Malone's antagonistic faction lacks distinct color identity or sigil; opposition is communicated through enemy unit placement and installation architecture rather than aesthetic branding.
